topics.title: Bioluminescence
topics.summary: Living things that make their own light, and the strange chemistry behind it.

prompts.body: Why do so many deep-sea creatures glow blue rather than red? What does that tell you about the water above them?

review.notes: Approved as-is. Considered swapping 'glow' for 'shine' but kept original.

prompts.body: Fireflies flash in patterns. Pick one species and find out what its rhythm means.

topics.title: Old Maps
topics.summary: What the world looked like to people who had never seen it from above.

prompts.body: Find a map from before 1800. Look only at the parts that are blank or filled with drawings. What did the maker not know?

trails.title: A short walk through living light
trails.summary: Two prompts that move from the deep sea to your backyard.

redirect_methods.summary: Let a short documentary lead you somewhere unexpected.
Open a long-form piece instead of the next short post.
A small puzzle to reset your attention loop.
Quiet minutes to notice what you wanted from the scroll.
Twenty undistracted minutes into something genuinely curious.

reflection_prompts.body: What were you actually looking for when you reached for the app?
Name one detail from today that pulled your attention back into the room.
What question are you carrying right now?
List five small things you noticed before opening another feed.
What topic keeps returning to you, even when you try to ignore it?
Write about one thing that stayed with you from the last few minutes.
Describe something you noticed today that was smaller than you expected.
What did you already know before today that you only just put into words?
If you stopped right here, what would you remember about this stretch of time?
Of everything you've taken in today, what would you actually like to keep?
Imagine a quieter version of the next hour. What would be in it?
